spec_match.rationale: Concept (per-tier eager/lazy/skip strategy) matches the spec's 'web apps adapting to device capability' framing, but the page hard-codes the tier from hardwareConcurrency + deviceMemory and never calls `navigator.cpu.performance()` — the surface this concept exists to demonstrate.

interactive.rationale: Tier toggle, simulated load sequence with per-resource progress bars and timing, eager/deferred/skipped summary, savings-vs-high-tier readout — a real interactive panel.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises a resource manifest, per-tier strategy, simulate-page-load with sequence playback, and a bytes-saved-vs-high summary — all delivered.

edge_cases.rationale: Skip-on-low and lazy/eager paths are shown for 14 modules, but no demo of: a deferred import() actually running on demand (only the eager bar animates), a network failure on a critical eager module, or a tier mismatch where the device says 'high' but bandwidth is constrained.

references.rationale: See-also section links to three sibling concepts but does NOT include the chromestatus.com/feature/5189864286978048 URL. This breaks the self-heal invariant in CLAUDE.md #3 and is the recurring regression flagged across this feature folder.

design_system.rationale: CSS variables are used throughout, button styling pairs background/color correctly, but several inline hex colours appear (`#edfdf0` load-yes, `#fdf8e8` load-lazy, `#f0f0ec` load-skip, `#e8eeff` load-eager) for the badge palette. Violates 'CSS variables, never raw hex' in CLAUDE.md #5.

openQuestions.title: Add the chromestatus link to see-also
openQuestions.detail: The see-also section breaks the self-heal invariant. Add `https://chromestatus.com/feature/5189864286978048` matching the sibling concept pages.

openQuestions.title: Actually read navigator.cpu.performance()
openQuestions.detail: The detection script uses only hardwareConcurrency + deviceMemory. Call the new API with feature detection, fall back to the heuristic if missing, and surface both in the 'Detected' banner so visitors see what the page is for.

openQuestions.title: Replace badge hex colours with variables
openQuestions.detail: load-yes/lazy/skip/eager badges use raw hex backgrounds. Either add accent variables to public/styles.css or swap to existing color-mix() patterns against --accent-emerald / --accent-amber (if added) / --text-muted.

openQuestions.title: Animate a deferred import on demand
openQuestions.detail: The simulate-load sequence only animates eager loads. Add a 'trigger deferred import' button next to each lazy badge that fires the bar animation when clicked, so visitors see what 'lazy' actually means at runtime.

summary: Conceptually well-designed manifest-driven loader, but it ships without the chromestatus link, never reads the new API, and uses raw hex colours for its badge palette. Address the three regressions and animate the deferred path to make it a complete demo.
